task: Harden the Blue Life Commons production first viewport with one real licensed, review-visible species record.
surface: Responsive public home page first viewport
audience: Public learners, citizen scientists, researchers, conservation organizations, educators, and open-data reusers
first_read: Blue Life Commons publishes source-linked, review-gated ocean-life records with visible rights and Git history.

references.label: Whale shark artifact
references.reason: Real record, sources, review state, rights, approved media, and reuse license

references.label: Public media guard
references.reason: Confirms approved public image and blocks candidate media

references.label: Blue Life brand pack
references.reason: Existing oceanic editorial tokens and brand posture

avoid: Implied scientific approval or hidden review state
direction.signature: One whale shark record exposes its source, rights, review, reuse, and Git history in the first viewport.
system: Tier A approved record and CC0 media
One source-trace motion with static reduced route
Visible media credit and artifact license

asset_quality.why_premium: The first viewport uses a real governed species record and its approved licensed media rather than decorative imagery.

artifacts.notes: Direct Chrome exports inspected at 1440 desktop, 390 mobile, reduced motion, and 320 CSS pixels at DPR 2 (640 physical pixels). The Tier A proof remains legible, credited, review-visible, and free of clipping or horizontal overflow.
Existing record inspected: status, sources, media rights, review checks, and reuse license are explicit.

checks.name: source-and-rights
checks.evidence: Whale shark record declares three sources; media is approved CC0 with 9/9 checks.

checks.name: review-honesty
checks.evidence: The surface derives science, ethics, editor, and artifact status from metadata; blanket ethics-reviewed and peer-reviewed claims were removed.

checks.name: public-outcomes
checks.evidence: The impact ledger publishes only approved or published claims and displays the current zero plus the number of claims still in review.

checks.name: security-and-build
checks.evidence: Frozen install, typecheck, 78-route production build, source/ethics/catalog/media validators, scoped image hosts, and zero-vulnerability npm audit passed.

checks.name: canonical-and-og
checks.evidence: Generated HTML has exact production canonicals and Open Graph URLs for home, contribution, impact, and whale-shark record routes; the approved CC0 whale-shark image is the Open Graph proof asset.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier A: the primary proof is the repository's real whale-shark record and approved CC0 media with source, creator, rights state, review state, and Git history. The added favicon is a direct Tier C application of the existing owned vector mark.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: System Chrome 1440 desktop, 390 mobile, reduced-motion, 320 CSS/640 physical reflow, Impact, Contribute, encyclopedia, and whale-shark exports were directly inspected; no clipping, incoherent overlap, broken proof media, or horizontal overflow was found.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: The first viewport has one dominant proof object and clear actions; mobile preserves proposition, actions, counts, proof image, rights, review state, and history in the intended order. The reduced-motion source trace rests at opacity 1 with no transform.

checks.name: accessibility-and-reflow
checks.evidence: Skip link is first in focus order with a 2px visible outline and moves focus to #main. Desktop focus order follows navigation, actions, license, record, source, and history. The mobile menu button exposes aria-expanded/aria-controls and opens from the keyboard. No horizontal overflow at 390 CSS pixels or 320 CSS pixels at DPR 2.

checks.name: runtime-and-metadata
checks.evidence: Home, Contribute, Impact, Encyclopedia, and whale-shark routes returned 200 with exact bluelifecommons.org canonical and Open Graph URLs, zero image failures, and zero console/page/request errors. The added brand icon returns 200 image/svg+xml.

checks.name: provisional-impact-language
checks.evidence: Unapproved records now label their metadata as a proposed impact claim and explicitly state that Hypercert eligibility metadata is neither issuance nor certification.

score.notes: 5 first read/hierarchy, 4 brand distinctiveness, 4 craft, 5 accessibility/responsiveness/reduced motion, 5 factual accuracy/provenance, and 5 intended-surface usefulness. Direct responsive inspection closes the previous score cap.

known_risks: The approved Vercel Blob mirror is preferred; Wikimedia remains the reviewed fallback and OG source.
The underlying species record still requires expert review; the UI must never imply otherwise.
Funding providers, grants, partner outreach, sponsorship, and Hypercert issuance remain separate human-governed actions.
Custom domain and production state remain out of scope.
